source.raw_ocr_text: v.t. #242 enjoy greatly: He is so hungry that he will relish plain food. (#4 TY, ERR EMA 得津津有味 。 | No one will fail to relish his recent novel. 没有人不会欣赏他新近发表的长篇小说的 。 #344 delight in

headline.core_en: to enjoy something greatly; enjoyment or a pleasing flavor

familiar_bridge.summary: enjoy 是普通“喜欢、享受”；relish 更强调带着兴致、津津有味地享受。

core.main: relish 既可作动词，也可作名词。作动词常接 food、challenge、opportunity、novel；作名词可指 enjoyment，也可指调味酱。GRE 语境多看 enjoy greatly。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 非常享受食物、作品、挑战或机会。

examples.sentence: He was so hungry that he relished even plain food.
examples.translation: 他太饿了，连普通食物都吃得津津有味。
examples.teaching_note: relish food 表示享受食物。

examples.sentence: She relished the opportunity to debate a difficult question.
examples.translation: 她很享受辩论一个难题的机会。
examples.teaching_note: relish an opportunity/challenge 常表示积极享受挑战。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 带着愉悦感欣赏小说、表演或经历。

examples.sentence: Readers will relish his recent novel for its wit and energy.
examples.translation: 读者会因为这部近作的机智和活力而喜爱它。
examples.teaching_note: relish a novel 对应原书“欣赏小说”的方向。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Many readers enjoyed the story.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: enjoy 普通；relish 更强调兴致和满足感。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: She likes spicy food.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: like 很轻；relish 更强。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: He delights in solving difficult puzzles.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: delight in 与 relish 接近，更强调乐在其中。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: She savored every bite of the meal.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: savor 强调慢慢品味；relish 强调强烈享受。

antonyms.example_sentence: He disliked the bitter taste.
antonyms.difference_note: dislike 与 relish 相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: She loathed the tedious routine.
antonyms.difference_note: loathe 比 dislike 更强烈。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The sandwich came with onion relish.
confusable_words.difference_note: relish 可作名词指调味酱，但本词条重点是“享受”。

word_family.note: 动名词或现在分词。
word_family.example_sentence: The relishing response surprised the audience.

word_family.note: 较少见，可帮助理解。
word_family.example_sentence: The relishable response surprised the audience.

related_words.note: 和食物/体验享受相关。
related_words.example_sentence: Her savor became important in the debate.

source.raw_ocr_text: v.t. BASE; 堵住 block or become blocked with an accumulation of thick, wet matter: The machine was clogged with grease. 这人台机器因为油污太多而堵住了 。 | The sudden increase of phone calls clogged the switchboard. 突然增加的电话妨碍了交换人台的正常工作

headline.core_en: to block or become blocked, especially by a buildup of material or traffic

familiar_bridge.summary: block 是普通“堵住”；clog 更有东西堆积起来，把管道、机器、道路或系统堵住的画面。

core.main: clog 常用于 drain、pipe、machine、road、switchboard、system。它既可以是油污、泥土堵住，也可以是电话、交通、信息把系统堵住。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 油污、泥、垃圾等积累后堵住管道、机器或通道。

examples.sentence: The machine was clogged with grease.
examples.translation: 这台机器被油污堵住了。

examples.sentence: Leaves clogged the drain after the storm.
examples.translation: 暴风雨后，树叶堵住了排水口。
examples.teaching_note: clog a drain 是自然搭配。

usage_groups.group_title: 交通、电话或信息让系统拥堵
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 大量车辆、电话或请求让系统运行不畅。

examples.sentence: The sudden increase in phone calls clogged the switchboard.
examples.translation: 突然增加的电话堵塞了交换台。

examples.sentence: Tourist buses clogged the narrow streets all afternoon.
examples.translation: 整个下午，旅游巴士堵塞了狭窄街道。
examples.teaching_note: clog roads/streets 可用于交通拥堵。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: A fallen tree blocked the road.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: block 最普通；clog 强调堆积造成堵塞。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Traffic jammed the bridge for hours.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: jam 常指卡住或交通堵塞。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Debris obstructed the entrance.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: obstruct 更正式，强调阻挡通道或进程。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Weeds choked the river channel.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: choke 有使通道被塞满、无法流通的画面。

antonyms.example_sentence: Workers cleared the drain before noon.
antonyms.difference_note: clear 是把堵塞物清掉。

antonyms.example_sentence: The plumber unblocked the pipe.
antonyms.difference_note: unblock 与 clog 直接相反。

confusable_words.example_sentence: She wore wooden clogs in the garden.
confusable_words.difference_note: clog 也可作名词指鞋，但本词条重点是堵塞。

word_family.note: 常见形容词。
word_family.example_sentence: The clogged response surprised the audience.

word_family.note: un- 表示反向，指清除堵塞。
word_family.example_sentence: The word unclog helped clarify the example.

word_family.note: 动名词或现在分词。
word_family.example_sentence: The clogging response surprised the audience.

related_words.note: 名词，帮助理解堵塞结果。
related_words.example_sentence: Her blockage became important in the debate.

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 认真负责的; 8) BAY (of a person) wishing to do what is right, especially to do one's work or duty well and thoroughly: a diligent and conscientious teacher 勤劳尽责的教师 | a conscientious listener 专心的听者 nullify AnAlifar v.t. FIA: 使无效; 使失效 make of no use or value; cancel out: An unhealthy diet will nullify the effects of training. 不健康的饮食会抵消训练的效果

headline.core_en: careful and thorough because one wants to do what is right

familiar_bridge.summary: careful 是“仔细”；conscientious 还多一层责任感，表示认真把事情做好。

core.main: conscientious 常形容 teacher、worker、student、listener，也可形容 work、effort。它强调细致、可靠、尽责。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 人做工作或履行职责时仔细、负责、不敷衍。

examples.sentence: She is a diligent and conscientious teacher.
examples.translation: 她是一位勤勉尽责的教师。
examples.teaching_note: conscientious teacher 强调工作认真、有责任心。

examples.sentence: The report reflects conscientious research rather than quick speculation.
examples.translation: 这份报告体现了认真负责的研究，而不是匆忙猜测。
examples.teaching_note: conscientious research 指细致可靠的研究。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The careful editor found several errors.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: careful 只说仔细；conscientious 还强调责任感。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: A diligent student reviews notes every week.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: diligent 强调勤奋；conscientious 强调认真尽责。

advanced_synonyms.meaning: 一丝不苟的；谨慎守原则的
advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The scientist was scrupulous in reporting the data.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: scrupulous 更强调严格守原则和细节。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The historian made meticulous notes.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: meticulous 强调细节非常精确。

antonyms.example_sentence: A careless mistake changed the result.
antonyms.difference_note: careless 与 conscientious 的细致负责相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The company was negligent in maintaining the equipment.
antonyms.difference_note: negligent 强调没有尽到责任。

confusable_words.example_sentence: He was conscious of the risk.
confusable_words.difference_note: conscious 是有意识；conscientious 是认真尽责。

word_family.note: conscientious 的责任感来自 conscience 这条线。
word_family.example_sentence: Her conscience became important in the debate.
She spoke conscientiously during the meeting.

word_family.note: 名词形式。
word_family.example_sentence: Her conscientiousness became important in the debate.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: vt， 归罪于; 归咎于; 归因于 to say, often unfairly, that someone is responsible for something or has a particular quality: They imputed their defeat in the war to the stupidity of the generals. $4 $e AS HE 4] RH. =| How dare you impute such monstrous intentions to me? REL RA SAF BY RE BB HB KK EHR?

headline.core_cn: 归咎于；归因于；把……归于某人
headline.core_en: to attribute responsibility, blame, or a quality to someone or something, often unfairly

familiar_bridge.summary: attribute 是“归因于”；impute 更常带有把责任、动机或坏品质归到某人身上的意味。

core.main: impute 常用结构是 impute A to B，把失败、动机、意图、品质归因到某人或某事。语气常偏正式，也可能带不公平指责。

usage_groups.group_title: 把责任或原因归到某人身上
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 说某人或某事应为某个结果负责，常有归咎意味。

examples.sentence: They imputed their defeat to the generals' incompetence.
examples.translation: 他们把失败归咎于将军们的无能。
examples.teaching_note: 这个表达很常见。

examples.sentence: Critics imputed the collapse to poor planning.
examples.translation: 批评者把崩溃归因于计划不周。
examples.teaching_note: 这里 impute 表示正式归因。

usage_groups.group_title: 把动机或品质加到某人身上
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 认为某人有某种意图或特质，常可能不公平。

examples.sentence: How dare you impute such motives to me?
examples.translation: 你怎么敢把这种动机强加到我身上？
examples.teaching_note: impute motives/intentions 常带被冤枉的感觉。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The author attributed the change to economic pressure.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: attribute 较中性；impute 常带归咎或不公平意味。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: They blamed the delay on bad weather.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: blame 更日常；impute 更正式。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Scholars ascribe the poem to an unknown author.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: ascribe 更书面，可用于作者、原因、品质。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The report assigned responsibility to senior officials.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: assign responsibility 与 impute responsibility 接近。

antonyms.example_sentence: The evidence exonerated the accused official.
antonyms.difference_note: exonerate 是免除责任；impute 是归责任。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The article impugned the minister's honesty.
confusable_words.difference_note: impugn 是质疑品格或真实性；impute 是把责任或动机归到某人身上。

word_family.note: 名词形式。
word_family.example_sentence: Her imputation became important in the debate.
The imputable response surprised the audience.

related_words.note: 理解 impute 的中性入口词。
related_words.example_sentence: The word attribute helped clarify the example.

familiar_bridge.summary: teenage 是日常“十几岁的”；adolescent 更正式，强调从儿童到成人的发育阶段。

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 怀疑的; 不相信的 not easily convinced; having doubts or reservations: She's highly skeptical of the researchers' claims. #3 F FH W WIE TR HF KZ skeptic /skeptik/ n. 怀疑者; 持怀疑态度的人 a person who questions or doubts something (such as a claim or statement): Skeptics have pointed out flaws in the researchers' methods. 怀疑者指出了研究方法上的缺陷 。 派生 skepticism /skeptisizam/ n. 怀疑 ) 怀疑态度 a skeptical attitude; doubt as to the truth of something: These claims were treated with skepticism. 这些说法遭到了人们的怀疑

headline.core_en: having doubts; not easily convinced

familiar_bridge.summary: doubt 是“怀疑”；skeptical 形容一个人对 claim、method、promise 等不轻易相信。

core.main: skeptical 常用 be skeptical of/about。它可以是理性的怀疑，也可能是对证据不足的保留态度。

usage_groups.group_title: 对说法或证据持怀疑态度
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 不轻易相信某个 claim、promise、method 或 explanation。

examples.sentence: She was highly skeptical of the researchers' claims.
examples.translation: 她对研究人员的说法非常怀疑。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: He gave an incredulous laugh at the explanation.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: incredulous 更强调难以置信。

antonyms.example_sentence: A credulous audience accepted the rumor quickly.
antonyms.difference_note: credulous 是容易相信。

antonyms.example_sentence: The judge was convinced by the evidence.
antonyms.difference_note: convinced 与 skeptical 的怀疑相反。

confusable_words.example_sentence: Skeptics pointed out flaws in the method.
confusable_words.difference_note: skeptic 是人；skeptical 是形容词。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The claims were treated with skepticism.
confusable_words.difference_note: skepticism 是名词。

word_family.example_sentence: The skeptic response surprised the audience.
Her skepticism became important in the debate.
She spoke skeptically during the meeting.

source.raw_ocr_text: n. (答应他人时的 ) 欣然; 热情; 爽快 brisk and cheerful readiness: accept the invitation with alacrity 爽快地接受邀请

headline.core_en: brisk and cheerful readiness to do something

familiar_bridge.summary: readiness 是“准备好、愿意”；alacrity 是很爽快、很积极地答应或行动。

core.main: alacrity 常用于 accept/respond/undertake with alacrity。它强调动作快，而且态度积极。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 愿意立刻做某事，带着热情和轻快感。

examples.sentence: She accepted the invitation with alacrity.
examples.translation: 她爽快地接受了邀请。
examples.teaching_note: 这个表达很常见。

examples.sentence: The volunteers responded with alacrity when help was needed.

examples.teaching_note: respond with alacrity 强调积极又迅速。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: He showed eagerness to begin the work.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: eagerness 强调想做；alacrity 还强调动作爽快迅速。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Her readiness to help impressed everyone.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: readiness 较普通；alacrity 更积极轻快。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The promptness of the reply surprised us.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: promptness 强调快；alacrity 同时强调乐意。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: They pursued the reform with zeal.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: zeal 强调热情和投入，不一定突出速度。

antonyms.example_sentence: He agreed with obvious reluctance.

antonyms.example_sentence: Her hesitation made the offer seem less sincere.
antonyms.difference_note: hesitation 强调迟疑。

source.raw_ocr_text: phr. 尤其是 in particular; notably: There is a great deal at stake, not least in relation to the environment. 这会涉及很多问题 ， 尤其是环境方面

headline.core_en: especially; notably among several important things

familiar_bridge.summary: especially 是“尤其”；not least 更常出现在正式句子里，强调“在很多重要因素中，这一个尤其值得提”。

core.main: not least 通常插在句中，用来突出一个原因、方面或影响。它不是比较大小的 least，而是强调重要性。

usage_groups.group_title: 在多个因素中特别强调一个
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 列举若干重要因素时，突出其中一个尤其重要。

examples.sentence: There is a great deal at stake, not least in relation to the environment.
examples.translation: 这件事牵涉甚广，尤其是在环境方面。
examples.teaching_note: not least in relation to... 用来突出一个重要方面。

examples.sentence: The policy failed, not least because it ignored local needs.
examples.translation: 这项政策失败了，尤其是因为它忽视了当地需求。
examples.teaching_note: not least because... 强调一个重要原因。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The plan is risky, especially for small firms.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: especially 更日常；not least 更正式、常作插入强调。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The book is useful, particularly for beginners.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: particularly 与 especially 接近。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Several countries objected, notably France.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: notably 常用于正式列举。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The report criticized several policies, in particular the tax plan.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: in particular 与 not least 接近，但不一定有“诸多重要因素之一”的感觉。

confusable_words.example_sentence: This is the least expensive option.
confusable_words.difference_note: least 单独是“最少/最不”；not least 是固定短语，表示尤其。

related_words.note: 日常入口表达。
related_words.example_sentence: She spoke especially during the meeting.

related_words.note: 更正式的列举表达。

source.raw_ocr_text: n. 支持者 a person who argues for or supports something: a leading proponent of sustainable development 可持续发展的主要支持者 _ 近义词 defender

headline.core_en: a person who supports or argues in favor of an idea, policy, or cause

familiar_bridge.summary: supporter 是普通“支持者”；proponent 更正式，常指公开支持某个理论、政策或方案的人。

core.main: proponent 常接 of：a proponent of sustainable development / reform / a theory。它强调支持并为之辩护或倡导。

usage_groups.group_title: 公开支持某个观点或方案的人
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 为某个理论、政策、改革或事业辩护、发声。
common_patterns: a leading proponent of sustainable development

examples.sentence: She became a leading proponent of sustainable development.
examples.translation: 她成了可持续发展的主要倡导者。
examples.teaching_note: 这个表达很常见。

examples.sentence: Proponents of the policy argue that it will reduce inequality.
examples.translation: 这项政策的支持者认为它会减少不平等。
examples.teaching_note: proponents argue that... 常用于议论文。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Supporters of the plan gathered outside city hall.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: supporter 普通；proponent 更正式，强调支持某个主张或方案。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: She is an advocate for prison reform.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: advocate 与 proponent 很接近，常有积极倡导的意味。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: He was a champion of free speech.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: champion 更带强烈支持和保护意味。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The scholar is a defender of classical education.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: defender 更强调为某事辩护。

antonyms.example_sentence: Opponents of the law challenged it in court.
antonyms.difference_note: opponent 与 proponent 直接相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: Critics of the proposal questioned its cost.
antonyms.difference_note: critic 强调提出批评。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The proposal gained support quickly.
confusable_words.difference_note: proposal 是被支持的方案；proponent 是支持方案的人。

word_family.note: proponent 常支持某个被 propose 的方案。
word_family.example_sentence: The word propose helped clarify the example.

word_family.note: 常与 proponent/opponent 一起出现。
word_family.example_sentence: The proposal response surprised the audience.

related_words.note: 同语义场。
related_words.example_sentence: They tried to advocate the old assumption.

extension.root_affix: pro- 在这里帮助理解“支持、赞成”的方向，如 pro-democracy 表示支持民主的。

source.raw_ocr_text: #474 ##X v.t. y--H speak or write in favor of (an action or person); attempt to justify: defend one's behavior 为某人的行为辩护 | defend an argument 为一个论点辩护派生 defensible /di'fensobl/ adj. 站得住脚的; 合理的 justifiable by argument: Slavery is not morally defensible. 奴隶制在道德上站不住脚 。 i£ 044 justifiable

headline.core_en: to protect someone or something; to argue in support of a person, action, or idea

familiar_bridge.summary: protect 是“保护”；defend 还可以指在争论中为某个行为、观点或人辩护。

core.main: defend 有两个常见方向：实际保护、防御；以及用理由为行为或论点辩护。原书这里重点是“辩护、证明合理”。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 用理由支持某个行为、论点或决定。

examples.sentence: He struggled to defend his behavior after the report was released.
examples.translation: 报告发布后，他很难为自己的行为辩护。
examples.teaching_note: defend one's behavior 是这个用法。

examples.sentence: The author defends the argument with historical evidence.
examples.translation: 作者用历史证据为这一论点辩护。
examples.teaching_note: defend an argument 表示证明论点站得住脚。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 保护人、地方、权利或制度免受攻击。

examples.sentence: The villagers organized to defend their homes.
examples.translation: 村民组织起来保卫家园。
examples.teaching_note: 这是 defend 的保护/防御义。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The law protects workers from exploitation.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: protect 强调防止伤害；defend 可保护也可辩护。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: She tried to justify the expense.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: justify 更直接说明合理性；defend 可在争论中为其辩护。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The new evidence vindicated his decision.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: vindicate 常表示事后证明正确。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The court upheld the earlier ruling.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: uphold 强调维护原则、法律或裁决。

antonyms.example_sentence: The article attacked the minister's record.
antonyms.difference_note: attack 与 defend 的方向相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The committee condemned the policy as unjust.
antonyms.difference_note: condemn 是公开否定或谴责。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The conclusion is defensible but not certain.
confusable_words.difference_note: defensible 是形容词，表示可以被 defend。

word_family.note: 形容观点或行为能被合理辩护。
word_family.example_sentence: The defensible response surprised the audience.
The word defense helped clarify the example.

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 防御的; 守势的; 被动防守的 used or intended to defend or protect: defensive actions 防御行为 adj. 急于反驭批评的; 认为别人在批评自己的; 心虚的 very anxious to challenge or avoid criticism: There's no need to get so defensive. I was only making a suggestion. 我就是提个建议 ， 你不用那么激动

headline.core_cn: 防御的；戒备的；急于自我辩护的
headline.core_en: intended to defend; or quick to react as if criticized

familiar_bridge.summary: defend 是“保护、辩护”；defensive 可以是实际防御，也可以指一个人一听到批评就急着自我辩护。

core.main: defensive 有两个常见义项：defensive actions 是防御行为；get defensive 是对批评过度敏感、急着反驳。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 行动、武器、策略等目的是防御而不是进攻。

examples.sentence: The army adopted a defensive strategy after losing ground.
examples.translation: 失去阵地后，军队采取了防御策略。
examples.teaching_note: defensive strategy 是军事或竞争语境。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 对建议或批评反应过度，好像别人正在攻击自己。

examples.sentence: There's no need to get so defensive; I was only making a suggestion.
examples.translation: 不用这么急着辩解；我只是提个建议。
examples.teaching_note: get defensive 是口语中非常常见的心理反应用法。

examples.sentence: His defensive tone made the apology sound less sincere.
examples.translation: 他防备性的语气让道歉听起来不那么真诚。
examples.teaching_note: defensive tone 指急于自我保护的语气。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The parent became protective during the interview.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: protective 偏保护；defensive 可指防御或急于辩解。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: She gave a guarded answer to the question.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: guarded 强调小心不暴露信息。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: His self-justifying speech convinced few listeners.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: self-justifying 专指为自己辩护。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The policy was reactive rather than strategic.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: reactive 强调被刺激后反应，不一定含自我辩护。

antonyms.example_sentence: The team shifted to an offensive strategy.
antonyms.difference_note: offensive 与防御义相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: Her open response reduced the tension.
antonyms.difference_note: open 可与心理防御姿态相反。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The argument is defensible.
confusable_words.difference_note: defensible 是可辩护；defensive 是防御性或急于辩解。

word_family.note: 对应动词。
word_family.example_sentence: The word defend helped clarify the example.
The word defense helped clarify the example.

word_family.note: 名词，指 defensive 的心理状态。
word_family.example_sentence: Her defensiveness became important in the debate.

source.raw_ocr_text: #4744 v.t. JE; 怀疑 feel or express doubt about; raise objections to: She was questioning my decision. 她在质疑我的决定 。 派生 questionable /kwestfansbl/ adj. 不可靠的; 不可信的;可疑的 doubtful as regards truth or quality: a questionable conclusion 不可靠的结论派生 unquestioning /An kwestfanm/ adj. 不加怀疑的 accepting something without dissent or doubt: an unquestioning acceptance 不加怀疑的接受

headline.core_en: to doubt or challenge something; a question or issue

familiar_bridge.summary: ask 是“问”；question 作动词时不是普通提问，而是质疑某个决定、说法或结论。

core.main: question 在本词条重点是动词“质疑”。它常接 decision、claim、authority、conclusion。派生词 questionable 和 unquestioning 很重要。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 对某个决定、说法、证据或结论表示怀疑。

examples.sentence: She was questioning my decision.
examples.translation: 她在质疑我的决定。
examples.teaching_note: question a decision 表示怀疑其合理性。

examples.sentence: The new evidence led researchers to question the conclusion.
examples.translation: 新证据使研究人员开始质疑这个结论。
examples.teaching_note: question the conclusion 是学术语境常见结构。

usage_groups.group_title: 作为名词表示问题或议题
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 可以指需要回答的问题，也可以指有争议的议题。

examples.sentence: The central question is whether the policy can be enforced fairly.
examples.translation: 核心问题是这项政策能否被公平执行。
examples.teaching_note: question 作名词可指议题。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The committee doubted the accuracy of the data.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: doubt 强调心里不相信；question 更像公开提出质疑。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The article challenges the old explanation.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: challenge 比 question 更主动、更有对抗性。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The lawyers disputed the witness's account.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: dispute 常带双方争论。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The police interrogated the suspect for hours.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: interrogate 是正式盘问，不等于普通 question。

antonyms.example_sentence: The audience accepted the explanation.
antonyms.difference_note: accept 是不再质疑。

antonyms.example_sentence: The committee endorsed the proposal.
antonyms.difference_note: endorse 表示公开支持。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The report rests on a questionable assumption.
confusable_words.difference_note: questionable 是形容词，表示值得被质疑。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The leader demanded unquestioning loyalty.
confusable_words.difference_note: unquestioning 表示不质疑。

word_family.note: 值得被 question。
word_family.example_sentence: The questionable response surprised the audience.

word_family.note: un- 表示否定，指不质疑。
word_family.example_sentence: The unquestioning response surprised the audience.

word_family.note: 提出问题或质疑的人。
word_family.example_sentence: Her questioner became important in the debate.

related_words.note: 动词义的基础入口。
related_words.example_sentence: The word doubt helped clarify the example.

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 充满活力的; 富有热情的 full of energy and enthusiasm: a vibrant cosmopolitan city 富有活力的国际化大都市

headline.core_en: full of energy, life, enthusiasm, or strong color

familiar_bridge.summary: lively 是“活泼的”；vibrant 更有生命力和热度，也可形容颜色鲜明。

core.main: vibrant 常形容 city、community、culture、market、color。它通常是正面词，强调活力、热情、丰富。

usage_groups.group_title: 城市、群体或文化充满活力
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 某个地方、群体或文化生机勃勃、活动丰富。

examples.sentence: The neighborhood has become a vibrant center of music and food.
examples.translation: 这个街区已经成了充满活力的音乐和美食中心。
examples.teaching_note: vibrant center/city/community 表示地方有生气。

examples.sentence: The novel captures the energy of a vibrant cosmopolitan city.
examples.translation: 这部小说捕捉了一个充满活力的国际化都市的能量。
examples.teaching_note: 对应原书 a vibrant cosmopolitan city 的方向。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 颜色、声音或视觉效果明亮、强烈、醒目。

examples.sentence: The painter used vibrant colors to make the market scene feel alive.
examples.translation: 画家使用鲜明的色彩，让市场场景显得生动。
examples.teaching_note: vibrant colors 是视觉义。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The lively discussion lasted for hours.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: lively 较普通；vibrant 更强调生命力和丰富感。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The energetic speaker held the audience's attention.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: energetic 多形容人或活动有精力。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The city has a dynamic technology sector.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: dynamic 强调变化和推动力。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Her vivacious manner made the interview enjoyable.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: vivacious 多形容人活泼有魅力。

antonyms.example_sentence: The room looked dull after years without repainting.
antonyms.difference_note: dull 可对应活力不足或色彩暗淡。

antonyms.example_sentence: The performance felt lifeless despite the music.
antonyms.difference_note: lifeless 与 vibrant 的生命力相反。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The phone vibrated on the desk.
confusable_words.difference_note: vibrate 是动词“振动”；vibrant 是形容词“有活力/鲜明”。

word_family.note: 词根画面是充满振动和能量。
word_family.example_sentence: They tried to vibrate the old assumption.
Her vibration became important in the debate.

word_family.note: 名词形式。
word_family.example_sentence: Her vibrancy became important in the debate.

related_words.note: 同语义场。
related_words.example_sentence: Her vivacity became important in the debate.

source.raw_ocr_text: n. 令人痛苦的事物 something that causes pain or suffering: He does not consider his deafness an affliction. 他并不把耳驮看作一种痛苦

headline.core_cn: 痛苦；折磨；令人痛苦的事
headline.core_en: a condition or problem that causes pain or suffering

familiar_bridge.summary: suffering 是“痛苦”；affliction 更像长期折磨人的疾病、困境或负担。

core.main: affliction 常用于 illness、deafness、poverty、grief。它不只是短暂不舒服，而是使人承受痛苦的状态或事物。

usage_groups.group_title: 疾病、残障或困境造成痛苦
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 某种状态长期给人造成痛苦或负担。

examples.sentence: He does not consider his deafness an affliction.
examples.translation: 他并不把自己的耳聋看作一种痛苦。
examples.teaching_note: affliction 指可能长期困扰人的状态。

examples.sentence: The disease was an affliction that shaped her entire childhood.
examples.translation: 这种疾病成了影响她整个童年的痛苦。
examples.teaching_note: disease/illness as an affliction 很自然。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The war caused great suffering.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: suffering 是痛苦本身；affliction 是造成痛苦的状态或事物。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Financial trouble forced them to move.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: trouble 更日常；affliction 更重、更正式。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The trial was a painful ordeal for the family.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: ordeal 强调艰难经历。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The essay treats corruption as a social malady.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: malady 可指疾病或社会弊病。

antonyms.example_sentence: The unexpected help was a blessing.
antonyms.difference_note: blessing 与 affliction 的痛苦负担相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The medicine brought relief from pain.
antonyms.difference_note: relief 是痛苦减轻。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The infliction of pain was deliberate.
confusable_words.difference_note: infliction 是施加痛苦的行为；affliction 是痛苦状态或事物。

word_family.example_sentence: The word afflict helped clarify the example.

word_family.note: 形容词或过去分词。
word_family.example_sentence: The afflicted response surprised the audience.

word_family.note: 较少见，作识别即可。
word_family.example_sentence: The afflictive response surprised the audience.

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 单调的; 乏味的 never changing and therefore boring: speak in a monotonous voice 声音单调地说话

headline.core_en: dull and boring because it never changes

familiar_bridge.summary: same 是“一样”；monotonous 是一直一样，所以让人觉得单调无聊。

core.main: monotonous 常形容 voice、routine、work、landscape、sound。它强调缺少变化造成乏味。

usage_groups.group_title: 声音、工作或景象单调无变化
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 重复、没有变化，因此令人厌烦。

examples.sentence: The lecturer spoke in a monotonous voice.
examples.translation: 讲师用单调的声音讲话。
examples.teaching_note: monotonous voice 是这个用法。

examples.sentence: The job was monotonous, involving the same task every hour.
examples.translation: 这份工作很单调，每小时都做同样的事。
examples.teaching_note: monotonous work/routine 强调重复乏味。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The movie was boring after the first scene.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: boring 很宽泛；monotonous 说明无聊来自缺少变化。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The lecture was dull and hard to follow.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: dull 可指沉闷；monotonous 更强调重复。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The tedious process took all afternoon.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: tedious 强调拖长导致厌烦。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The work was repetitive but easy.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: repetitive 强调重复本身，不一定评价强烈。

antonyms.example_sentence: The course offers varied assignments.
antonyms.difference_note: varied 与 monotonous 的无变化相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The engaging speaker kept the audience alert.
antonyms.difference_note: engaging 强调让人投入。

confusable_words.example_sentence: He answered in a monotone.
confusable_words.difference_note: monotone 是名词，指单调音调；monotonous 是形容词。

word_family.note: 帮助理解 mono- 的单一。
word_family.example_sentence: The word monotone helped clarify the example.
The word monotony helped clarify the example.

related_words.note: mono- 迁移词，表示一个人说。
related_words.example_sentence: The word monologue helped clarify the example.

root_affix.note: 帮助理解 monotonous 里‘单一、缺少变化’的感觉。

source.raw_ocr_text: n. 惊险小说; 惊悚片; 悬疑片 a novel, play, or movie with an exciting plot, typically involving crime or espionage: a political thriller 政治悬疑小说

headline.core_cn: 惊悚小说；惊险片；悬疑片
headline.core_en: a novel, play, or movie with an exciting plot, often involving crime or espionage

familiar_bridge.summary: thrill 是“兴奋、刺激感”；thriller 是让人紧张兴奋的小说、电影或戏剧。

core.main: thriller 常用于 political thriller、crime thriller、spy thriller。它是作品类型，不是“令人兴奋的人”。

usage_groups.group_title: 情节紧张刺激的作品类型
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 小说、电影或戏剧以悬念、犯罪、间谍或危险情节吸引读者。

examples.sentence: The novel is a political thriller about corruption inside the government.
examples.translation: 这部小说是一部关于政府内部腐败的政治惊悚小说。

examples.sentence: The film works as a thriller because every scene raises the stakes.
examples.translation: 这部电影之所以像惊悚片，是因为每一幕都提高了风险。
examples.teaching_note: thriller 指作品类型。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The mystery begins with a missing letter.

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The suspense story keeps readers uncertain.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: suspense story 强调悬念感。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The espionage novel follows a double agent.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: espionage novel 是 thriller 常见子类型。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The memoir became an unexpected page-turner.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: page-turner 强调让人停不下来读，不一定是惊悚题材。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The performance was thrilling.
confusable_words.difference_note: thrilling 是形容词；thriller 是作品类型名词。

related_words.note: thriller 的核心效果之一。

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 令人兴奋的; 激动人心的 exciting: She gave a thrilling performance. 她作了扣人心弦的表演 。 WR. thrill /9rd/ n. 兴奋; 激动 a sudden feeling of excitement and pleasure: the thrill of jumping out of an airplane 跳伞的激动

headline.core_cn: 令人兴奋的；扣人心弦的
headline.core_en: very exciting, often because of danger, suspense, or strong emotion

familiar_bridge.summary: exciting 是普通“令人兴奋的”；thrilling 更有心跳加速、紧张刺激的感觉。

core.main: thrilling 常形容 performance、game、journey、discovery、finale。它强调强烈的兴奋或刺激。

usage_groups.group_title: 表演、比赛或经历令人兴奋
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 某件事带来强烈兴奋、刺激或悬念。

examples.sentence: She gave a thrilling performance that held the audience silent.
examples.translation: 她作了一场扣人心弦的表演，让观众屏息凝神。

examples.sentence: The final minutes of the game were thrilling.
examples.translation: 比赛最后几分钟令人激动。
examples.teaching_note: thrilling 常用于比赛和戏剧性经历。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The trip was exciting for the children.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: exciting 普通；thrilling 更强烈、更刺激。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The dramatic rescue was shown on television.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: dramatic 强调戏剧性或冲突。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The climb was exhausting but exhilarating.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: exhilarating 更强调精神振奋。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The singer gave an electrifying performance.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: electrifying 强度很高，像让现场沸腾。

antonyms.example_sentence: The speech was dull and predictable.
antonyms.difference_note: dull 与 thrilling 相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The tedious meeting lasted three hours.
antonyms.difference_note: tedious 强调拖长造成厌烦。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The movie is a political thriller.
confusable_words.difference_note: thriller 是名词，指作品类型。

word_family.note: 名词/动词。
word_family.example_sentence: The victory gave the crowd a thrill.

word_family.note: 形容人的感受。

Her thriller became important in the debate.

source.raw_ocr_text: n. ”因果关系; 因果性 the relationship between cause and effect: Scientists found no causality between the events. 科学家没有在这些事件之间发现因果关系 。 派生 causal /ko:zal/ adj. 原因的; 有因果关系的 relating to or acting as a cause: the causal factors associated with illness 造成疾病的因素

headline.core_en: the relationship between cause and effect

familiar_bridge.summary: cause 是“原因”；causality 是更抽象的“因果关系”本身。

core.main: causality 常用于科学、哲学、统计和历史解释。它强调 A 是否真的导致 B，而不只是两件事同时出现。

usage_groups.group_title: 判断是否存在因果关系
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 研究两个事件之间是否真的有 cause-and-effect 关系。

examples.sentence: Scientists found no causality between the two events.
examples.translation: 科学家没有发现这两个事件之间存在因果关系。

examples.sentence: The study shows correlation, but it does not establish causality.
examples.translation: 这项研究显示相关性，但没有建立因果关系。
examples.teaching_note: GRE/学术阅读常区分 correlation 和 causality。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The lesson explains cause and effect in ecosystems.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: cause and effect 是更直观的表达；causality 更抽象。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The cause of the delay was unclear.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: cause 是原因；causality 是原因与结果之间的关系。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The data suggest a causal relationship between stress and illness.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: causal relationship 是更完整的学术表达。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The correlation does not prove causation.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: correlation 是相关，不等于 causality。

antonyms.example_sentence: The timing was a coincidence, not evidence of causality.
antonyms.difference_note: coincidence 表示没有因果关系。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The accident caused several casualties.
confusable_words.difference_note: casualty 与 causality 形近但意思不同。

word_family.example_sentence: The causal response surprised the audience.

word_family.note: 名词，和 causality 接近。
word_family.example_sentence: Causation is harder to prove than correlation.

word_family.note: 基础词。
word_family.example_sentence: The word cause helped clarify the example.

related_words.note: 常和 causality 对比。
related_words.example_sentence: The data showed correlation, not causation.

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 独裁的; 专制的 favoring or enforcing strict obedience to authority, especially that of the government, at the expense of personal freedom: an authoritarian leader 独裁主义的领导人 | an authoritarian government 独裁政府 n. 专治的人; 独裁的人 an authoritarian person: His father was a strict authoritarian. (i & % 严厉而专制 。 派生 authoritarianism /a,0ore'tesrron/ n. 专治; 独裁 the enforcement or advocacy of strict obedience to authority at the expense of personal freedom: arguments in defence of authoritarianism 为专制独裁辩护的说法

headline.core_en: favoring strict obedience to authority at the expense of personal freedom

familiar_bridge.summary: authority 是“权威、权力”；authoritarian 是权力压过个人自由的专制倾向。

core.main: authoritarian 可形容 leader、government、regime、style，也可作名词指专制者。要和 authoritative 区分：authoritative 是可信有权威，不是专制。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 强调服从权威，压制个人自由或不同意见。

examples.sentence: The country was governed by an authoritarian leader who tolerated no dissent.
examples.translation: 这个国家由一位不容异议的专制领导人统治。
examples.teaching_note: authoritarian leader 强调权力压制自由。

examples.sentence: The reforms were criticized for strengthening authoritarian rule.
examples.translation: 这些改革因加强威权统治而受到批评。
examples.teaching_note: authoritarian rule 是政治语境常见表达。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The teacher was strict but fair.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: strict 可中性；authoritarian 带专制、压制自由的负面意味。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The manager's controlling style frustrated the team.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: controlling 可用于个人风格；authoritarian 更政治或权力化。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The company was run in an autocratic manner.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: autocratic 与 authoritarian 接近，强调一人或少数人掌权。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The despotic ruler silenced critics.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: despotic 更强，带暴虐意味。

antonyms.example_sentence: The group adopted a democratic decision-making process.
antonyms.difference_note: democratic 强调参与和自由。

antonyms.example_sentence: The policy reflected liberal values.
antonyms.difference_note: liberal 强调自由和开放，与威权相对。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The dictionary is an authoritative source.
confusable_words.difference_note: authoritative 是可信、有权威；authoritarian 是专制。

word_family.example_sentence: Authoritarianism limits public criticism.

word_family.note: 基础词。
word_family.example_sentence: The judge had authority to reject the request.

word_family.note: 少见，识别即可。
word_family.example_sentence: They tried to authoritarianize the old assumption.

related_words.note: 同政治语义场。
related_words.example_sentence: Her autocracy became important in the debate.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 权威性的; 可信的 able to be trusted as being accurate or true; reliable: an authoritative dictionary 具有权威性的词典

headline.core_cn: 权威性的；可信的；有把握的
headline.core_en: reliable and trusted as accurate; or showing confident authority

familiar_bridge.summary: authority 是“权威”；authoritative 是“有权威、可信”。它不是 authoritarian 的“专制”。

core.main: authoritative 常形容 dictionary、source、account、study，也可形容 tone/voice 表示有把握。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 来源准确可靠，值得信任。

examples.sentence: The article cites an authoritative dictionary to clarify the term.
examples.translation: 文章引用权威词典来澄清这个术语。

examples.sentence: Her book became the authoritative account of the crisis.
examples.translation: 她的书成了这场危机的权威叙述。
examples.teaching_note: authoritative account 表示最可靠、最被认可的说明。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 说话方式自信、有把握，让人觉得可信。

examples.sentence: He spoke in an authoritative tone without sounding arrogant.
examples.translation: 他说话语气很有权威感，但并不傲慢。
examples.teaching_note: authoritative tone 是信服感，不是专制。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The report is reliable because the data are public.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: reliable 较普通；authoritative 还带专业权威。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The witness seemed trustworthy.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: trustworthy 可形容人或信息可信。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The study is a definitive account of the period.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: definitive 强调最终、最具决定性。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The explanation was credible but incomplete.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: credible 表示可相信，不一定有权威地位。

antonyms.example_sentence: The source was unreliable and often inaccurate.
antonyms.difference_note: unreliable 与 authoritative 的可信相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The conclusion rests on questionable evidence.
antonyms.difference_note: questionable 表示值得怀疑。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The regime became increasingly authoritarian.
confusable_words.difference_note: authoritarian 是专制；authoritative 是权威可信。

word_family.note: 基础词。
word_family.example_sentence: The judge had authority to reject the request.
She spoke authoritatively during the meeting.

word_family.note: 易混词，需区分。
word_family.example_sentence: The word authoritarian helped clarify the example.

related_words.note: 语义近邻。
related_words.example_sentence: The credible response surprised the audience.

source.raw_ocr_text: vt， 授权; 批准 give official permission for or approval to (an undertaking or agent): We are willing to authorize the president to use force if necessary. 我们愿意授权总统在必要的时候使用武力 。 还又启 endorse

headline.core_en: to give official permission or approval

familiar_bridge.summary: permit 是“允许”；authorize 更正式，强调由有权力的人或机构正式批准。

core.main: authorize 常用于 authorize someone to do something、authorize payment/use/action。它和 endorse 不同：endorse 是公开支持，不一定有正式批准权。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 有权机构给某人做某事的正式许可。
common_patterns: authorize the president to use force

examples.sentence: Congress authorized the president to use force if necessary.
examples.translation: 国会授权总统在必要时使用武力。
examples.teaching_note: 这个表达很常见。

examples.sentence: Only the director can authorize the payment.
examples.translation: 只有主管可以批准这笔付款。
examples.teaching_note: authorize payment 表示正式批准。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The rules permit visitors to enter after ten.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: permit 较普通；authorize 更正式，含有权力来源。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The board approved the budget.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: approve 与 authorize 接近，但 authorize 更强调给予权限。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The committee sanctioned the new procedure.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: sanction 作动词可表示正式批准，也可表示制裁，需看语境。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The law empowers local officials to act quickly.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: empower 强调给予权力或能力。

antonyms.example_sentence: The policy forbids the use of private data.
antonyms.difference_note: forbid 与 authorize 相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The law prohibits smoking in public buildings.
antonyms.difference_note: prohibit 更正式。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The senator endorsed the candidate.
confusable_words.difference_note: endorse 是公开支持，不一定正式授权。

word_family.example_sentence: The team needed authorization before entering the site.
Only authorized staff could open the file.

word_family.note: 基础词。
word_family.example_sentence: The judge had authority to reject the request.

source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 倒退的; 退步的 reverting to an earlier and inferior condition: a retrograde policy 倒行逆施的政策

headline.core_en: returning to an earlier and worse condition

familiar_bridge.summary: backward 是“向后的”；retrograde 更正式，常批评政策、态度或变化倒退到更差的旧状态。

core.main: retrograde 常形容 policy、step、movement、attitude。它带负面评价，强调从进步方向倒退。

usage_groups.group_title: 政策或态度倒退到更差状态
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 做法、观点或社会变化回到更落后、更糟的状态。

examples.sentence: The law was criticized as a retrograde step for civil rights.
examples.translation: 这项法律被批评为民权方面的倒退。
examples.teaching_note: retrograde step/policy 是常见批评表达。

examples.sentence: The proposal reflected retrograde attitudes toward education.
examples.translation: 这项提案反映了对教育的倒退态度。
examples.teaching_note: retrograde attitudes 表示观念落后。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The policy seemed backward compared with recent reforms.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: backward 较普通；retrograde 更正式、更批判。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The tax was criticized as regressive.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: regressive 与 retrograde 接近，常用于政策或社会效果。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The party adopted a reactionary platform.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: reactionary 更政治化，强调反对进步。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The novel depicts violence as an atavistic impulse.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: atavistic 更书面，强调退回原始状态。

antonyms.example_sentence: The city adopted a progressive housing policy.
antonyms.difference_note: progressive 与 retrograde 直接对照。

antonyms.example_sentence: The report offered a forward-looking plan for energy policy.
antonyms.difference_note: forward-looking 强调面向未来。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The law had retroactive effect.
confusable_words.difference_note: retroactive 是追溯适用；retrograde 是倒退。

word_family.example_sentence: The policy marked a retrogression in civil rights.

word_family.note: 同 retro- 方向，但不是负面倒退。
word_family.example_sentence: In retrospect, the warning signs were obvious.

root_affix.note: 帮助理解 retrograde 的‘向后倒退’和 retrospect 的‘回顾’。

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: adj， 自发的; 自主的; 非外在原因导致的 (of a process or event) occurring without apparent external cause: spontaneous growth of wood 森林的自然生长 adj. ( 人或举止 ) 自然的; 无拘无束的 (of a person) having an open, natural, and uninhibited manner: spontaneous behavior 自然的举止派生 spontaneity /sponte'neroti/ n. 自发; (举止等的 ) 自然 the condition of being spontaneous; spontaneous behavior or action: They rallied to my support with remarkable spontaneity. 他们非常自觉地一起来支持我 。 | She acted with an extraordinary spontaneity. 她戏演得非常自然

headline.core_en: happening naturally without being forced, planned, or caused from outside

familiar_bridge.summary: natural 是“自然的”；spontaneous 更强调不是被安排、逼出来的，而是自己发生或自然流露。

core.main: spontaneous 可以说事件“自发发生”，也可以说人的行为“自然、不拘束”。两个方向都围绕“没有外力强迫”。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 某个过程、变化或反应没有明显外部推动，自己发生。

examples.sentence: The scientist studied the spontaneous growth of plants in the abandoned field.
examples.translation: 科学家研究了废弃田地里植物的自然生长。
examples.teaching_note: 这里强调生长不是人为栽培出来的。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 人说话或行动自然流露，不显得排练过或拘束。

examples.sentence: Her spontaneous laughter made the conversation feel relaxed.
examples.translation: 她自然流露的笑声让谈话显得轻松。
examples.teaching_note: 用于人时，spontaneous 常带正面色彩。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: His natural reaction was to laugh.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: natural 范围更宽；spontaneous 更强调未经计划或外力推动。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The trip was unplanned but enjoyable.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: unplanned 只说没有计划；spontaneous 往往还带自然流露的感觉。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Her instinctive response was to protect the child.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: instinctive 强调出于本能。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: He gave an impromptu speech after dinner.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: impromptu 多指临时即兴的表演、讲话或安排。

antonyms.example_sentence: The pause was deliberate, not accidental.
antonyms.difference_note: deliberate 强调有意识、有计划。

antonyms.example_sentence: His smile looked forced.
antonyms.difference_note: forced 与自然流露相反。

confusable_words.example_sentence: Participation in the study was voluntary.
confusable_words.difference_note: voluntary 强调出于自愿；spontaneous 强调自然发生或自然流露。

word_family.example_sentence: Her spontaneity made the interview feel genuine.
The audience applauded spontaneously.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: 2474S. vi. (一群人 ) 共同支持;团结在 … 周围 to bring or come together for a common purpose bring or come together in order to support a person or cause or for concerted action: Many people in the community have rallied to the mayor's side. 当地很多人站在了市长一边 。 #4 rallying cry n. ( 将人们团结在一起来支持某人或某事的 ) 口号 a word or phrase that is used to make people join together to support an idea, cause, etc.: “We believe!” became the rallying cry of the fans. “我们相信 ! ”成为了粉丝们的集结号

headline.core_cn: 团结起来支持；重新振作；集会
headline.core_en: to come together in support, or to recover strength after a setback

familiar_bridge.summary: support 是“支持”；rally 更有“一群人聚到一起支持某人/某事”或“从低谷重新起来”的动作感。

core.main: rally 在原书中重点是“聚集起来支持”。它还常表示人、市场、队伍从低迷中恢复。

usage_groups.group_title: 人们团结起来支持某人或事业
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 一群人为了共同目标站到一起，支持某个人、观点或行动。

examples.sentence: Many people in the community rallied to the mayor's side.
examples.translation: 社区里很多人站到了市长一边。
examples.teaching_note: rally to someone's side 表示公开站队支持。

usage_groups.group_title: 从低谷中恢复或重新振作
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 队伍、市场或人的精神状态在受挫后重新变强。

examples.sentence: The team rallied after halftime and won the game.
examples.translation: 球队在中场休息后重新振作，赢下了比赛。
examples.teaching_note: 这里不是“集会”，而是恢复状态。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 作为名词，rally 可指支持某个政治、社会目标的集会。

examples.sentence: The speech became a rallying cry for young voters.
examples.translation: 这场演讲成了年轻选民的动员口号。
examples.teaching_note: rallying cry 是把人团结起来的口号。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Several groups supported the reform.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: support 是普通“支持”；rally 强调聚集起来支持。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The economy slowly recovered after the crisis.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: recover 可对应 rally 的“恢复”义。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The campaign mobilized thousands of volunteers.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: mobilize 强调组织和动员人力。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: After the setback, the activists regrouped.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: regroup 强调受挫后重新整理队伍。

antonyms.example_sentence: The crowd dispersed after the announcement.
antonyms.difference_note: disperse 与聚集起来相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The stock collapsed after the report.
antonyms.difference_note: collapse 与市场/状态 rally 的恢复相反。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The critic railed against the policy.
confusable_words.difference_note: rail against 是“抨击”，不要和 rally 混淆。

word_family.note: 常见于 rallying cry。
word_family.example_sentence: The rallying response surprised the audience.

source.raw_ocr_text: v.i. 抱怨; 发牢骚 complain or protest about something in a bad-tempered but typically muted way: grumble about the quality of goods #22. & i A 4

headline.core_en: to complain in a quiet, bad-tempered way

familiar_bridge.summary: complain 是“抱怨”；grumble 更像小声、不满、带情绪地嘟囔。

core.main: grumble 不一定是正式投诉，更多是低声抱怨、发牢骚，语气不耐烦但通常不激烈。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 因为价格、质量、安排等不满而低声抱怨。

examples.sentence: Customers grumbled about the poor quality of the goods.
examples.translation: 顾客抱怨商品质量太差。
examples.teaching_note: 这个表达很常见。

examples.sentence: Several passengers began to grumble when the train was delayed again.
examples.translation: 火车再次延误时，几位乘客开始发牢骚。
examples.teaching_note: 强调不满但不是正式抗议。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Residents complained about the noise.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: complain 最普通；grumble 更低声、更带坏脾气。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: He muttered something under his breath.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: mutter 强调小声说话，不一定是抱怨。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The staff groused about the new schedule.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: grouse 较口语，和 grumble 很接近。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The audience murmured in disapproval.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: murmur 可指群体低声不满。

antonyms.example_sentence: The reviewer praised the restaurant's service.
antonyms.difference_note: praise 与抱怨相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: She accepted the delay without protest.
antonyms.difference_note: accept 强调不抱怨地接受。

confusable_words.example_sentence: Workers protested against the cuts.
confusable_words.difference_note: protest 更公开、更强烈；grumble 更小声、日常。

word_family.example_sentence: Her grumbler became important in the debate.

word_family.note: 名词或形容词。
word_family.example_sentence: The grumbling response surprised the audience.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: n. Far; 中立态度 the state of not supporting or helping either side in a conflict, disagreement, etc.; impartiality: declare neutrality 宣布中立 | maintain strict neutrality 严守中立近义词 impartiality

headline.core_en: the state of not supporting either side in a conflict or disagreement

familiar_bridge.summary: neutral 是“中立的”；neutrality 是这种“不偏向任何一方”的状态或原则。

core.main: neutrality 常出现在战争、争论、调查、媒体报道等场景中，强调不站队、不帮助任何一方。

usage_groups.group_title: 冲突或争论中保持中立
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 不支持冲突或争论中的任一方。

examples.sentence: The country declared neutrality at the beginning of the war.
examples.translation: 战争开始时，这个国家宣布中立。
examples.teaching_note: declare neutrality 是正式政治语境。

examples.sentence: Judges are expected to maintain strict neutrality during a trial.
examples.translation: 法官在审判中应保持严格中立。
examples.teaching_note: neutrality 也可用于制度、公正性语境。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The judge's impartiality was essential to the trial.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: impartiality 强调判断公正；neutrality 强调不站队。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The rules were designed to ensure fairness.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: fairness 更普通，范围更宽。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The nation pursued nonalignment during the conflict.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: nonalignment 多用于国际政治。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The report was praised for its objectivity.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: objectivity 强调判断基于事实而非个人立场。

antonyms.example_sentence: The article showed a clear bias against the policy.
antonyms.difference_note: bias 与 neutrality 的不偏不倚相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: Partisanship weakened the committee's credibility.
antonyms.difference_note: partisanship 强调强烈站队。

confusable_words.example_sentence: His indifference to the issue angered the group.
confusable_words.difference_note: neutrality 是不偏向；indifference 是不关心。

word_family.example_sentence: A neutral judge must not favor either side.

word_family.note: 同族动词，但意思转向“让作用变中性/失效”。
word_family.example_sentence: They tried to neutralize the old assumption.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: #4740 X vt， 抵消 … 的效果; 使失效 render (something) ineffective or harmless by applying an opposite force or effect: Rising prices neutralized increased wages. 物价上涨抵消了工资上涨带来的好处

headline.core_en: to make something ineffective or harmless by counteracting it

familiar_bridge.summary: cancel out 是“抵消”；neutralize 更正式，常说某种力量、影响或危险被另一种力量抵掉。

core.main: neutralize 的核心不是“保持中立”，而是让某个效果、威胁或优势失去作用。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 一个因素把另一个因素的效果抵掉。

examples.sentence: Rising prices neutralized the benefit of higher wages.
examples.translation: 物价上涨抵消了工资上涨带来的好处。
examples.teaching_note: 对应强调效果被抵掉。

examples.sentence: The new evidence neutralized the opponent's strongest argument.
examples.translation: 新的证据抵消了对手最强的论点。
examples.teaching_note: 论证语境中也可用 neutralize。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 让酸、毒物、武器或威胁不再有伤害作用。

examples.sentence: The chemical can neutralize the acid before it damages the surface.
examples.translation: 这种化学物质可以在酸损伤表面之前将其中和。
examples.teaching_note: 科学语境中常有“中和、无害化”的意思。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Exercise can counteract some effects of stress.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: counteract 与 neutralize 很接近，强调用相反作用抵消。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The gains offset the earlier losses.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: offset 常用于数值、损失和收益的抵消。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: A later agreement nullified the earlier contract.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: nullify 更强调法律或逻辑上使无效。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: One flaw can negate the value of the experiment.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: negate 更抽象，可指使价值或论证失效。

antonyms.example_sentence: The heat intensified the smell.
antonyms.difference_note: intensify 与 neutralize 的削弱/抵消相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The media amplified the public reaction.
antonyms.difference_note: amplify 强调扩大影响。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The mediator remained neutral during the dispute.
confusable_words.difference_note: neutral 是状态；neutralize 是让某作用失效。

word_family.note: 基础形容词。
word_family.example_sentence: A neutral judge must not favor either side.
Her neutrality became important in the debate.
Neutralization reduced the acid's effect.

source.raw_ocr_text: #74 X v.t. (derive sth. from) 从 … 获得; 由 … 得到 obtain something from (a specified source): The river derives its name from an Indian chief. 那条河因一个印第安疯长而得名 。 | He derived his enthusiasm for literature from his father. 他对文学的爱好受他父亲影响 。 v.i. (derive from) X11; 出自; 由 … 而来 arise from or originate in (a specified source): Much of the book's appeal derives from the personality of its central character. 这本书的魅力主要来 HFEAAWER.

headline.core_en: to come from a source, or to obtain something from a source

familiar_bridge.summary: come from 是“来自”；derive 更正式，常说名字、想法、利益、快乐等“源自/得自”某个来源。

core.main: derive 的核心是 source：某物从某个来源而来，或某人从某事中获得某种东西。

usage_groups.meaning_focus: 名字、词语、想法、特征等来自某个来源。

examples.sentence: The river derives its name from an Indian chief.
examples.translation: 那条河的名字源自一位印第安酋长。
examples.teaching_note: derive its name from 是原书核心结构。

examples.sentence: Many English words are derived from Latin or Greek.
examples.translation: 许多英语词源自拉丁语或希腊语。
examples.teaching_note: be derived from 常用于词源或来源。

usage_groups.group_title: 从某事中获得感受或好处
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 从活动、关系或经历中获得快乐、力量、灵感等。

examples.sentence: He derived his enthusiasm for literature from his father.
examples.translation: 他对文学的热情来自父亲的影响。
examples.teaching_note: derive A from B 表示 A 的来源。

examples.sentence: The community derives much of its income from tourism.
examples.translation: 这个社区的大部分收入来自旅游业。
examples.teaching_note: derive income/benefit from 很自然。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Her confidence comes from years of practice.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: come from 最日常；derive from 更正式。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The researchers obtained data from several archives.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: obtain 强调取得；derive 强调从某来源取得或产生。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The custom originated in rural communities.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: originate 强调起点或起源。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The conflict stemmed from a misunderstanding.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: stem from 常用于问题、结果或现象的来源。

antonyms.example_sentence: The engine generates electricity.
antonyms.difference_note: generate 强调产生来源本身；derive 强调从来源而来。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The policy deprived many families of support.
confusable_words.difference_note: deprive 形近但意思完全不同。

word_family.example_sentence: The derivation of the word is uncertain.

word_family.note: 形容词或过去分词。
word_family.example_sentence: Derived forms often preserve part of the original meaning.

word_family.note: 同族词，另有“缺乏原创性”的评价义。
word_family.example_sentence: The derivative response surprised the audience.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: adj. 派生的; 衍生的; 非原创的 made up of or marked by derived elements: a derivative term 派生词 adj. 老套的; 没有新意的 lacking originality; banal: a highly derivative prose style 毫无创意的散文风格

headline.core_cn: 派生的；衍生的；缺乏原创性的
headline.core_en: derived from something else; lacking originality

familiar_bridge.summary: derive 是“源自”；derivative 就是“从别的东西派生出来的”，在艺术评论里常带“没新意”的批评。

core.main: derivative 有中性的“派生/衍生”义，也有负面的“模仿别人的、缺乏原创性”义。

usage_groups.group_title: 从别的形式或来源派生出来
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 词语、概念、产品等由另一个东西发展而来。

examples.sentence: The technical term is derivative of an older Latin word.
examples.translation: 这个技术术语源自一个更古老的拉丁词。
examples.teaching_note: 这里 derivative 是中性的“派生的”。

usage_groups.group_title: 作品或风格缺乏原创性
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 文章、音乐、电影等明显模仿已有模式，显得老套。

examples.sentence: The critic called the novel derivative because its plot copied familiar formulas.
examples.translation: 评论家认为这部小说缺乏原创性，因为情节照搬熟悉套路。
examples.teaching_note: 艺术评论中 derivative 常是负面评价。

examples.sentence: His prose was polished but highly derivative.
examples.translation: 他的散文很流畅，但非常缺乏原创性。
examples.teaching_note: 对应原书 a highly derivative prose style 的方向。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The design looked copied from an earlier model.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: copied 更直接；derivative 更正式，可指风格缺乏原创性。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The conclusion felt unoriginal.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: unoriginal 是最直接的评价。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The painting was technically skilled but imitative.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: imitative 强调模仿他人风格。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The film followed a formulaic plot.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: formulaic 强调按套路写。

antonyms.example_sentence: The essay offered an original interpretation of the poem.
antonyms.difference_note: original 与 derivative 的负面义相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The company introduced an innovative design.
antonyms.difference_note: innovative 强调新方法或新想法。

confusable_words.example_sentence: The dictionary explains the derivation of the word.
confusable_words.difference_note: derivation 是名词“来源/派生”；derivative 是形容词或名词。

word_family.example_sentence: The derive response surprised the audience.
Derived forms often preserve part of the original meaning.
The derivation of the word is uncertain.

source.source_note: Local OCR from split unit PDF; source wording retained where OCR was readable.
source.raw_ocr_text: v.t. #84t; 取代 to take the place of (someone or something that is old, no longer useful, etc.); to replace (someone or something): Steam locomotives were superseded by diesel. 蒸汽机车被柴油机车取代 。 | New ways of thinking superseded older ones. 新的思想方法取代较旧的思想方法

headline.core_en: to replace something older, less useful, or no longer current

familiar_bridge.summary: replace 是“替换”；supersede 更正式，常说新技术、新制度、新想法取代旧的。

core.main: supersede 的重点是“后来者让旧事物过时或不再适用”，常带时代更新的感觉。

usage_groups.group_title: 新技术、制度或想法取代旧的
usage_groups.meaning_focus: 新的东西出现后，旧的东西不再被使用或不再适用。

examples.sentence: Steam locomotives were superseded by diesel engines.
examples.translation: 蒸汽机车被柴油机车取代。
examples.teaching_note: 这个表达很常见。

examples.sentence: New ways of thinking gradually superseded older ones.
examples.translation: 新的思维方式逐渐取代了旧的思维方式。
examples.teaching_note: 可用于思想、理论、方法的更新。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: The new system replaced the old one.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: replace 最普通；supersede 更正式，强调旧事物过时。

basic_synonyms.example_sentence: Digital records took over from paper files.
basic_synonyms.difference_note: take over 更口语，可指接管或替代。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: The new theory supplanted the older explanation.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: supplant 与 supersede 接近，常指后来者替代原有事物。

advanced_synonyms.example_sentence: Automation displaced many routine jobs.
advanced_synonyms.difference_note: displace 强调把原来位置上的东西挤走。

antonyms.example_sentence: The museum preserved the old machines.
antonyms.difference_note: preserve 与取代旧物相反。

antonyms.example_sentence: The school retained the traditional curriculum.
antonyms.difference_note: retain 强调继续保留原有事物。

confusable_words.example_sentence: She succeeded him as editor.
confusable_words.difference_note: succeed 可指继任；supersede 强调替代并使旧事物不再适用。

word_family.note: 名词，较正式。
word_family.example_sentence: The supersession of old rules took years.

word_family.note: 过去分词或形容词。
word_family.example_sentence: The superseded policy remained in the archive.
